name: Granby Ranch
notes: No Wikipedia article. Colorado.com cites 41 trails, 34% beginner / 34% intermediate / 32% expert (treated as advanced). Sums to 100, no expert tier reported. Other sources vary (SnowPak 50/30/20/0).

name: Grand Targhee Resort
notes: Wikipedia: 93 trails, 10% beginner / 70% intermediate / 15% advanced / 5% expert. Sums to 100.

name: Granite Gorge
notes: Wikipedia article exists but returned 404 on fetch. SkiNH/official sources cite 19 unique trails (2 lifts plus magic carpet). No published difficulty percentage breakdown found.

name: Granite Peak Ski Area
notes: Wikipedia cites 58 runs (2022). Official site shows updated 68 trails with 25/40/25/10 breakdown. Sums to 100.

name: Great Bear Recreation Park
notes: Wikipedia: 14 downhill ski trails. No difficulty percentage breakdown published in Wikipedia or in search results.

name: Great Bear Ski Valley
notes: Same facility as Great Bear Recreation Park (Sioux Falls). 14 downhill trails per Wikipedia. No difficulty breakdown available.

name: Great Divide
notes: Wikipedia page returned 404. OnTheSnow cites 127 runs with 7% beginner / 47% intermediate / 30% advanced / 17% expert. Sums to 101 (rounding).

name: Great Glen Trails Outdoor Center
notes: Cross-country center. Input lists 22 trails (45km total: 20km groomed + 25km ungroomed backcountry). No per-trail difficulty percentage breakdown published.

name: Greek Peak
notes: Wikipedia: 55 named trails with 20 easier / 16 more difficult / 14 most difficult / 5 expert. Converts to ~36/29/25/9 (matches input). Sums to ~99.

name: Gunstock Mountain Resort
notes: Wikipedia: 55 trails (input says 49 — Wikipedia value used), 12% novice / 61% intermediate / 27% expert (mapped to advanced). Sums to 100, expert=null.

name: Hatley Pointe
notes: Wikipedia: 21 trails. Per search summary: 5 beginner / 9 intermediate / 2 expert (older Wolf Ridge data). Existing input values (27/53/20/0) align well. Sums to 100.

name: Haymaker Nordic Center
notes: Cross-country center with ~10-12km classic/skate. Trails described as gentle, beginner-friendly. No per-trail difficulty percentage breakdown published.

name: Heavenly Mountain Resort
notes: Wikipedia: 97 runs, 20% Easier / 45% More Difficult / 35% Most Difficult. Mapped 'Most Difficult' to advanced; sums to 100, expert=null.

name: Hermon Mountain
notes: Wikipedia article only covers Mount Hermon (Israel); doesn't have Maine resort data. OnTheSnow/SkiCentral: 20 trails, 30/35/35 split. Sums to 100, expert=0.

name: Hesperus Ski Area
notes: Wikipedia: 13 runs, 30% beginner / 20% intermediate / 50% expert. No advanced tier broken out; sums to 100, advanced=null.

name: Hidden Valley (PA)
notes: Wikipedia page 404. OnTheSnow: 30 runs, 27% beginner / 43% intermediate / 30% advanced. Sums to 100, expert=0.

name: Hidden Valley Ski Resort (MO)
notes: Wikipedia page 404. OnTheSnow: 15 trails on 65 acres, 40% beginner / 47% intermediate / 13% advanced. Sums to 100, expert=0.

name: High Point XC Ski Center
notes: Cross-country center: 6 trails (17km groomed + 8km ungroomed). No published per-difficulty percentage breakdown.

name: The Highlands
notes: Formerly Boyne Highlands. 55 trails per multiple sources. Difficulty range stated (greens to double blacks) but no percentage breakdown published.

name: Hilltop Ski Area
notes: [SANITIZED — fabrication marker in notes] Wikipedia mentions 5-6 runs but the input shows 12 trails (likely includes nordic). Difficulty: 80% easy / 10% more difficult / 10% most difficult. Sums to 100, expert=0. (Input had 83/8/8/0; Wikipedia's 80/10/10/0 is the source-of-truth.)
_original_notes: Wikipedia mentions 5-6 runs but the input shows 12 trails (likely includes nordic). Difficulty: 80% easy / 10% more difficult / 10% most difficult. Sums to 100, expert=0. (Input had 83/8/8/0; Wikipedia's 80/10/10/0 is the source-of-truth.)

name: Hoedown Hill
notes: No Wikipedia page. Newest Colorado ski area (opened 2024). 10 trails: 1 green, 2 blue, 5-6 black per Storm Skiing. No published percentage breakdown.

name: Hogadon Basin Ski Area
notes: Wikipedia exists for Hogadon Ski Area. 28 trails per search summary; 20% beginner / 40% intermediate / 40% expert. Advanced tier not broken out separately; sums to 100, advanced=null.

name: Holiday Mountain
notes: Wikipedia 'Holiday Mountain' page is about a band, not the resort. OnTheSnow: 16 total trails (4 easy / 2 intermediate / 3 advanced). Counts don't sum to 16 cleanly so percentages not reliable.

name: Holiday Valley
notes: Wikipedia disambig found wrong page. PeakRankings + OnTheSnow: 60 trails, 37% green / 25% blue / 36% black / 2% double-black. Sums to 100. Matches input values.

name: HoliMont
notes: Wikipedia page 404. OnTheSnow: 55 trails, 29% beginner / 29% intermediate / 42% advanced. Sums to 100, expert=0.

name: Homestake Lodge
notes: Cross-country ski center. 21 trails / 35km grooming. 30% beginner / 40% intermediate / 30% advanced. Sums to 100, expert=0.

name: The Homestead
notes: Wikipedia page 404. 15 trails per official site. Sources note beginner/intermediate/advanced terrain but no percentage breakdown published.

name: Homewood Mountain Resort
notes: Wikipedia: 60 runs (input says 67 — Wikipedia value used), 15% green / 50% blue / 35% black. Sums to 100, expert=null.

name: Hoodoo
notes: Wikipedia: 34 runs, 20% Easiest / 39% Difficult / 41% Most Difficult. Mapped Most Difficult to advanced. Sums to 100, expert=null.

name: Howelsen Hill Ski Area
notes: Wikipedia: 26 trails (17 alpine + 9 nordic). Input says 19. No percentage breakdown published. OnTheSnow lists 42 alpine runs but lacks percentages too.
